sections.a_description: The distinctive visual language of Aurora UI — combining gradient, glow, neon elements to create interfaces that are immediately recognizable and purposefully designed. Every color, font choice, and spacing decision works together to evoke the specific mood and cultural context that defines this aesthetic, from the primary accent color to the carefully chosen typography.

This style finds its natural home across websites and applications that value gradient and glow design principles. You'll see it on creative portfolios, brand microsites, specialized platforms, and digital experiences where the visual language itself communicates as powerfully as the content — proving that the right aesthetic choice can transform a standard layout into a memorable experience.

a_image_cover.alt: Aurora UI style use case illustration
Aurora UI style use case illustration
Aurora UI style use case illustration

sections.a_description: The distinctive visual aesthetic of Aurora UI — a design approach that creates unique, memorable interfaces. Background: dark (#050520). The .inner panel has a slightly lighter background with subtle border treatment and appropriate border-radius. Colors: #22d3ee as the primary accent for interactive elements and highlights. #d0e0f8 for headings, #7a8ab0 for body text. The aesthetic captures the essence of aurora ui through careful color choices, spacing, and visual weight. Set in Sora — chosen for its visual character that complements this design style. Heading at weight 600-700, 24-28px, line-height 1.2-1.3. Eyebrow at weight 500, 11px, uppercase, 2-3px letter-spacing, color #22d3ee. Body at weight 400, 14-15px, line-height 1.6-1.7, color #7a8ab0. Buttons: primary — #22d3ee background with contrasting text. Secondary: transparent with #22d3ee border. Alternatives: system-ui (universal fallback), sans-serif stack.
